en: Guard your steps when you go to the house of God. To draw near to listen is better than to offer the sacrifice of fools, for they do not know that they are doing evil.
zh: 你到 神的殿，腳步要謹慎；近前聆聽，勝於愚昧人獻祭，因為他們不知道所作的是惡的。

en: Be not rash with your mouth, nor let your heart be hasty to utter a word before God, for God is in heaven and you are on earth. Therefore let your words be few.
zh: 在 神面前不可冒失開口，心急發言，因為 神在天上，你在地上，所以你的言語要寡少。

en: For a dream comes with much business, and a fool's voice with many words.
zh: 掛慮多就令人作夢，言語多就顯出愚昧。

en: When you vow a vow to God, do not delay paying it, for he has no pleasure in fools. Pay what you vow.
zh: 你向 神許了願，就不可遲遲不還，因為他不喜悅愚昧人；所許的願必須償還。

en: It is better that you should not vow than that you should vow and not pay.
zh: 許願不還，不如不許。

en: Let not your mouth lead you into sin, and do not say before the messenger that it was a mistake. Why should God be angry at your voice and destroy the work of your hands?
zh: 不可任你的口使自己犯罪，也不可在使者前面說是許錯了。何必使 神因你的聲音發怒，破壞你手中的工作呢？

en: For when dreams increase and words grow many, there is vanity; but God is the one you must fear.
zh: 多夢和多話，都是虛空的，你只要敬畏 神。

en: If you see in a province the oppression of the poor and the violation of justice and righteousness, do not be amazed at the matter, for the high official is watched by a higher, and there are yet higher ones over them.
zh: 如果你在一省之中，看到窮人遭受欺壓，公正和公義被奪去，也不必因此驚訝，因為高位者之上有較高的照應，在他們之上還有更高的。

en: But this is gain for a land in every way: a king committed to cultivated fields.
zh: 各人都從土地得著利益，就是君王也得到田地的供應。

en: He who loves money will not be satisfied with money, nor he who loves wealth with his income; this also is vanity.
zh: 貪愛銀子的，不因有銀子滿足；貪愛財富的，也不因得利知足。這也是虛空。

en: When goods increase, they increase who eat them, and what advantage has their owner but to see them with his eyes?
zh: 財物增加，吃用的人也增加，物主除了眼看以外，還有甚麼益處呢？

en: Sweet is the sleep of a laborer, whether he eats little or much, but the full stomach of the rich will not let him sleep.
zh: 勞力的人無論吃多吃少，都睡得甜；財主的豐足，卻不容他安睡。

en: There is a grievous evil that I have seen under the sun: riches were kept by their owner to his hurt,
zh: 我看見日光之下有一令人痛心的憾事，就是財主積聚財富，反受其害。

en: and those riches were lost in a bad venture. And he is father of a son, but he has nothing in his hand.
zh: 遭遇禍患，財富就盡失；他即使生了兒子，也沒有甚麼留給他。

en: As he came from his mother's womb he shall go again, naked as he came, and shall take nothing for his toil that he may carry away in his hand.
zh: 他怎樣從母胎赤身而來，也必怎樣赤身而去；在勞碌中得來的，他的手也帶不走甚麼。

en: This also is a grievous evil: just as he came, so shall he go, and what gain is there to him who toils for the wind?
zh: 他怎樣來，也要怎樣去，這也是令人痛心的憾事；他為風勞碌，有甚麼益處呢？

en: Moreover, all his days he eats in darkness in much vexation and sickness and anger.
zh: 他終生在黑暗中吃喝，多有愁煩、疾病與憤怨。

en: Behold, what I have seen to be good and fitting is to eat and drink and find enjoyment in all the toil with which one toils under the sun the few days of his life that God has given him, for this is his lot.
zh: 我認為既善又美的，就是人在 神所賜給他一生有限的年日中吃喝，享受他在日光之下勞碌所得的一切，因為這是他的分。

en: Everyone also to whom God has given wealth and possessions and power to enjoy them, and to accept his lot and rejoice in his toil- this is the gift of God.
zh: 蒙 神賜予財富與資產的人， 神都使他能夠享用，並取自己的分，在勞碌中自得其樂，這是 神的恩賜。

en: For he will not much remember the days of his life because God keeps him occupied with joy in his heart.
zh: 他不多思念自己一生的年日，因為 神使喜樂充滿他的心。
